titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Francis Smyth to Augustine Birrell, 27 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Francis Smyth

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the MP for South Leitrim, Thomas Francis Smyth (1875-1937), to Chief Secretary for Ireland Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). Smyth is writing the letter to give his backing to Mr Thomas Delany (1868-1939), the crown solicitor for Longford, for the newly vacant post of chief crown solicitor of Ireland. The post became vacant following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/6258/10

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mohill,
County Leitrim,
Ireland March 27th 1916 Dear Mr Birrell, Mr.
J. W. Delany, Crown
Solicitor, Longford,
is a candidate for
the position of chief
Crown Solicitor for Ire-
land, vacated by the
death of Sir Malachy
Kelly. Mr Delany
has done a good deal
in connection with
recruiting in Ireland,
and I trust you may
see your way to
support him for the
position. Thanking you in
anticipation. Yours very truly, Thos. F. Smyth. The Right Hon,
A Birrell, P.C., M.P.,
Irish Office,
Old Queen Street,
London.
